nl_statement: If f is convex on s and has gradient f'(x) at every x ∈ s, then f(x) + ⟨f'(x), y - x⟩ ≤ f(y) for all x, y ∈ s.
lean_signature: theorem convex_first_order_condition_grad_depth2_idx1 {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E] {s : Set E} {f : E → ℝ} {f' : E → E} (hfun : ConvexOn ℝ s f) (h : ∀ x ∈ s, HasGradientAt f (f' x) x) : ∀ x ∈ s, ∀ y ∈ s, f x + inner ℝ (f' x) (y - x) ≤ f y
assumptions: f has gradient f' on s
reuse_reason: first-order characterization of convexity in gradient form — used pervasively
